name: Beef and Onion Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Leftover beef (preferably from Pot-au-feu or roast beef) sliced as thinly as possible
3 medium Yellow onions, sliced thinly and separated into rings
1 tbsp Red wine vinegar, to taste
Stock/Pot-au-Feu broth or canned beef broth
1 Sprig fresh thyme (or substitute 1 tsp dried)
recipeInstructions:
1. Ensure the beef is chilled for easy slicing. Slice as thinly as possible and set aside.
2. In a large skillet, melt butter over medium heat. Add onions and sauté until golden and very soft (about 10 minutes).
3. Sprinkle onions with enough flour to form a light roux. Stir for 2-3 minutes.
4. Add vinegar and mustard. Continue stirring. Pour in stock or Pot-au-Feu broth, adding more if needed, to create a thin sauce. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Arrange meat slices on top of the onions, overlapping them as desired. Spoon sauce over the meat.
6. Cover the dish and bake in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 30 minutes if using poached beef, or 60 minutes if using roast beef. Ensure the sauce does not reduce too much; add stock or water if necessary.
7. Remove from the oven and sprinkle with fresh bread crumbs mixed with a little butter to form a crust. Return to the oven for an additional 20-30 minutes until golden.
8. Garnish with parsley and lemon juice before serving.
Variations: Add tomatoes, garlic, mushrooms, or substitute vinegar with grated horseradish. Replace some stock with wine for added depth of flavor.
